Amaryllis belladonna is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is known for its large, showy flowers and is often used as an ornamental plant.

The Amaryllis belladonna plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. Its extracts have also been reported to have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects.

The bioactive constituents of Amaryllis belladonna include alkaloids such as galantamine, lycorine, and homophylline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Galantamine, in particular, has been found to have potential in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
